data.title: Nixxy names Julia Yu CFO
data.description:
• Nixxy (NIXX) on Thursday said it has appointed Julia Yu as Chief Financial Officer, replacing Adam Yang, whose consulting role has ended.
  • The company said, Yu brings experience in public-company finance, regulatory compliance, capital markets and financial operations. She has held senior roles at ExxonMobil, Unilever and several technology firms.
  • NIXX -0.0% premarket to $1.14.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Netflix leading bidder for Warner Bros. Discovery - CNBC
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) is the leading bidder for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), according to a CNBC report. Shares of Netflix edged lower by 0.7%.  Warner Bros. fell 1.8%.

Netflix (NFLX) is bidding 85% in cash and 15% in stock for Warner Bros. Discovery, (WBD) CNBC's David Faber said. 

The bidding for Warner Bros. (WBD) is likely to end early next week, according to Faber. Netflix is competing with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) and Comcast for the entertainment giant. 

Bloomberg reported late Wednesday that Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has more than doubled the proposed breakup fee in its offer to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to $5 billion, as part of a sweetened proposal designed to outshine rival bids.

data.title: Marriott expects RevPAR at lower end of guidance on soft U.S. trends
data.description:
Shares of Marriott International (MAR) were under pressure into Thursday’s open after the company warned that revenue per available room ((RevPAR)) may come in at the lower end of its outlook of 1% to 2% growth.

The company attributed the softer estimate “overwhelmingly” to trends in the U.S.

The remarks were made at Barclays 11th annual Eat, Sleep, Play, Shop Conference in New York.

Early last month, Marriott (MAR) updated its 2025 financial outlook to reflect the termination of its licensing agreement with Sonder following the latter’s default.

As Sonder’s short-term rentals are no longer available for booking on Marriott (MAR) channels or affiliated with the Bonvoy loyalty program, Marriott (MAR) now expects net rooms growth forecast to approach 4.5% from an earlier estimate of 5%.

When the hotel chain announced its third quarter results, it narrowed its full-year revenue guidance to a range of $5.395B and $5.415B from $5.365B and $5.420B and its profit guidance to a range of $9.98 to $10.06 per share from $9.85 to $10.08 per share.

Shares last traded down 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Tronox lenders sign cooperation pact after company revenue drops - Bloomberg
data.description:
Some creditors to Tronox (TROX) have signed a cooperation agreement that binds them together if the chemicals company wants to engage in discussions over its debt load, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Tronox (TROX) had more than $3 billion of long-term debt as of September 30, according to regulatory filings, and its $741 million term loan due in 2029 was quoted at $0.79 on the dollar Wednesday, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

The company is facing competition, weak demand, downstream destocking, lower sales volume, and pricing pressure, and Q3 revenues fell 13% Y/Y to $699 million while adjusted EBITDA plunged 48% to $74 million from $143 million in the year-earlier quarter.

data.title: Mark Cuban calls for end to generic drug application fees to boost domestic manufacturing
data.description:
• Mark Cuban, the billionaire businessman and founder of the online discount pharmacy Cost Plus Drugs, is urging the Trump administration to drop the regulatory fees required for generic drug applications as a way to increase domestic production of those medicines.
  • The US FDA currently charges of fee of $360K for each generic drug application. Cuban told Reuters that if that fee was waived, Cost Plus Drugs would start manufacturing high-cost generic drugs, such as rare disease treatments.
  • "There's no reason for us to manufacture a $4 drug that you get at Walmart, but if there's something that costs $100 or more, and we can manufacture it here and sell it for less, all the better," he said.
  • Cost Plus Drugs already operates a compounding facility in Dallas, and Cuban noted that it could manufacture generic drugs within a year if the fees are ended.

data.title: Genesco slips 29% after Q3 results and full-year guidance miss expectations
data.description:
Shares of Genesco (GCO) slipped nearly 29% during pre-market trading on Thursday as the company missed Q3 revenue and EPS estimates and reduced its full-year outlook.

The company lowered its total sales guidance to be up about 2%, down from prior guidance of 3% to 4%, versus estimated year-over-year growth of 3.51%. Comparable sales are expected to be up about 3% compared to fiscal 2025, down from the prior expectation of 4% to 5%.

The company also reduced its adjusted diluted earnings-per-share guidance from continuing operations to $0.95, compared with the previous range of $1.30 to $1.70, versus a consensus estimate of $1.55.

Mimi E. Vaughn, Genesco's Board Chair, President and Chief Executive Officer, said, “there are some factors causing us to moderate our view on the remainder of the year in spite of our current momentum. We have materially changed our sales and margin projections for Schuh to reflect the ongoing difficult U.K. market and performance. We have also moderated the growth assumptions for our other businesses based on the lower footwear consumer traffic and spending patterns we’ve recently witnessed on non-peak shopping days.”

Sandra Harris, Genesco's Senior Vice President of Finance and Chief Financial Officer, added, “Based primarily on the margin pressure at Schuh and our more cautious view on sales, we are revising our full year outlook and now expect adjusted earnings per share of approximately $0.95. We remain focused on tight cost control to offset some of this pressure and strong execution as we navigate these trends.”

For the third quarter, the firm reported revenue of $616.22 million, missing estimates by $1.48 million, while non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 missed estimates by $0.07.

The company did not repurchase any shares during the third quarter of fiscal 2026, and the guidance assumes no further share repurchases and a tax rate of 34%, excluding the tax impact of OBBBA and up from the prior 29% rate.

data.title: Nucor promotes Steve Laxton to president and COO
data.description:
• Steel producer Nucor (NUE) on Thursday said Stephen Laxton, Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President, has been promoted to the role of President and Chief Operating Officer effective January 1, 2026. 
  • David Sumoski, who has served as COO since 2021, will retire effective June 13, 2026. He has joined Nucor in 1995 as an electrical supervisor at Nucor Steel Berkeley, later serving as Maintenance Manager. He has served as Vice President and General Manager of Nucor Steel Marion and Nucor Steel Memphis before being named Executive Vice President in 2014. 
  • Laxton began his career with Nucor in 2003 as General Manager of Business Development and was promoted to Vice President in 2014. He has served as Nucor's CFO and Executive Vice President since 2022. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce reports higher provision for credit losses in FQ4
data.description:
Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CM) (CM:CA) was trading lower as the lender logged higher provision for credit losses in the fiscal fourth quarter.

The Toronto-based bank reported Q4 non-GAAP EPS of C$2.21, which beats the consensus estimate by C$0.13. Revenue of C$7.58B (+14.5% Y/Y) beats by C$320M.

Net interest income rose to C$4.13B from C$3.63B. Non-interest income advanced to C$3.44B from C$2.98B.

A C$11M (C$8M after-tax) amortization and impairment of acquisition-related intangible assets negatively impacted the quarterly results to the degree of C$0.01 per share.

Notably, CIBC set aside C$605M in provisions during FQ4, up 44% from the previous year and compared to C$559M in the quarter before.

The increase in provision for credit losses on performing loans was attributed to "an unfavourable change in the economic outlook in Canada and unfavourable credit migration in the current quarter and favourable model parameter updates in the same quarter last year.

Provision for credit losses on impaired loans was raised over higher provisions in all strategic business units, except U.S. commercial banking and wealth management.

Return on common shareholders' equity fell to 14.1% during the quarter from 14.2% at the end of FQ3. Common equity Tier 1 ratio was down to 13.3% from 13.4%.

CM shares were -1.77% Thursday pre-market to $85.30.

On the positive side, Canadian Imperial Bank raised its quarterly dividend by 10.3% from C$0.97 to C$1.07.

data.title: Astec signs agreement to acquire CWMF
data.description:
• Astec Industries (ASTE) on Thursday announced the signing of an agreement to acquire CWMF.
  • CWMF is a manufacturer of portable and stationary asphalt plant equipment and parts with annual revenues of approximately $50 million, with its distribution of products primarily concentrated in the Midwest, South-Central and Great Lakes regions of the United States.
  • The company anticipates closing on the acquisition in the first quarter of 2026.
  • "We expect the transaction to be accretive from day one and project our consolidated net leverage ratio to remain within our previously disclosed range of 1.5 to 2.5x net debt/adjusted EBITDA," said CFO Brian Harris.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Volaris reports load factor of 85% for November 2025
data.description:
• Controladora Vuela Compañía de Aviación (VLRS) said in November, Volaris’ ASM capacity increased 5.8%, while RPMs for the month grew 3.9%.
  • Mexican domestic RPMs increased 0.6%, while international RPMs rose 9.2%. Consolidated load factor decreased by 1.5 percentage points year-over-year to 85.3%.
  • During the month, Volaris transported 2.7 million passengers.
  • VLRS -1.38% premarket to $7.82.
  • Source: Press Release
